access_mechanism_type: formal_procurement (monitoring affects EPS-PBS supply) — monitoring system itself is not an access mechanism but a regulatory transparency tool
procurement_channels: Not procurement directly. Monitoring covers: EPS-PBS dispensing, MIPRES non-PBS, hospital pharmacy, retail pharmacy.
key_actors: MINSALUD (Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social) — designs SISCAATS (Sistema de Información para la Centralización de Alertas de Abastecimiento de Tecnologías en Salud). INVIMA — operates current 'Desabastecimientos' module since May 1, 2018. Superintendencia Nacional de Salud — sanctions for non-compliance. Superintendencia de Industria y Comercio (SIC) — competition aspects. Defensoría del Pueblo — tracks PQRS complaints and tutela patterns. ACEMI, ACEMI/AFIDRO trade associations — manufacturer reporting.
formulary_coverage: Monitoring covers all medicines, medical devices, biologics, medical gases, radiopharmaceuticals — including all oncology drugs (cisplatin, doxorubicin, carboplatin, trastuzumab) regardless of PBS status.
judicial_access_volume: Health tutelas: ~265,000 (2024) → ~312,500 (2025); ~24% related to medication denial. PQRS (Peticiones, Quejas, Reclamos y Solicitudes) for non-delivery of medications hit 52,655 in January 2025 (historical monthly peak).
registration_authority: INVIMA operates current shortage tracker; MINSALUD designing SISCAATS.
registration_backlog: Not applicable directly; INVIMA backlog of 14,000+ pending registrations is a separate but contributing factor (delays new market entrants that would relieve shortages).
reform_status: proposed (SISCAATS draft resolution open for public comment late 2025). EPS, IPS and pharmaceutical managers have 2 months to adapt; manufacturers, importers, wholesalers have 6 months to comply once enacted; medical device reporting fully integrated within 1 year.
currency_mechanism: Floating COP — not central to monitoring system design but contextual.
payer_debt_crisis: Indirectly tracked — system aims to detect financial-blockade-driven shortages where COP 4.2 trillion pharma debt restricts distributor supply.
shortage_rate: INVIMA Dec 2024 status: 8 medicines in shortage, 5 at risk, 181 under monitoring. By 2024 year-end, 13 pharmaceutical products were declared in shortage or near-shortage state. PQRS for non-delivery: 52,655 in Jan 2025 (monthly peak).
shortage_causes: Primary causes per MINSALUD/INVIMA: (1) EPS-IPS-distributor financial blockade (debt-driven), (2) manufacturer commercial issues / product reformulation (e.g., Etomidato), (3) INVIMA registration backlog, (4) global API supply chain disruptions, (5) market concentration of distributors, (6) political-reform uncertainty.
shortage_monitoring_system: Current: INVIMA 'Desabastecimientos' centralized module (since May 1, 2018), with public dashboard showing data through August 2025. New: SISCAATS in development by MINSALUD — three reporting modules (immediately available units, projected six-month units, distributed units), weekly reporting for immediate availability, plus event-based notification for production failures or quality issues. Non-compliance = violation of General System of Social Security in Health, sanctioned by SuperSalud / INVIMA / SIC depending on actor and severity. Saludata (Bogotá) and Defensoría also publish parallel indicators.
oncology_specific_shortage_events: [uncertain] — Dec 2024 INVIMA list of 8 acute shortages did not name cisplatin, doxorubicin, carboplatin or trastuzumab specifically. Patient-reported chemotherapy disruptions in 2024-2025 documented by Defensoría del Pueblo, People's Health Movement, but not formally classified as oncology-specific shortage event.
source_citation: 1) MINSALUD, 'No hay desabastecimiento generalizado de medicamentos en el país,' 2025, https://www.minsalud.gov.co/CC/Noticias/2025/Paginas/no-hay-desabastecimiento-generalizado-de-medicamentos-en-el-pais.aspx. 2) MINSALUD, 'Abastecimiento de Medicamentos,' https://www.minsalud.gov.co/salud/MT/paginas/desabastecimiento.aspx. 3) INVIMA, 'Desabastecimientos - Medicamentos y Productos Biológicos,' https://www.invima.gov.co/productos-vigilados/medicamentos-y-productos-biologicos/desabastecimientos. 4) Infobae, 'Ministerio de Salud buscará crear nuevo sistema de monitoreo de alertas por desabastecimiento de medicamentos,' Nov 27, 2025. 5) La FM, 'MinSalud alista nuevo sistema de monitoreo de alertas por desabastecimiento de medicamentos,' 2025. 6) El Colombiano, 'El año cierra con ocho medicamentos desabastecidos y cinco cerca de escasear,' Dec 2024. 7) Saludata Bogotá, indicators.
source_type_granular: government_primary (MINSALUD, INVIMA) + investigative_news (Infobae, El Colombiano, La FM)
